demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage (Aetna) and confirmed address in Atlantis matches the SNF face sheet. Discovered the phone number listed was a disconnected landline; updated Atlantis with the patient's current cell phone number and her daughter's (POA) contact info.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed the goal is Home with Home Health Agency (HHA) support for post-chemo weakness and medication management.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient is meeting PT goals for gait stability. SW indicates discharge is targeted for Friday, pending the final oncology clearance. PN educated the family on the Medical Assistant (MA) 'First Visit' role.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into Martha's iPhone; physical 'Safe Transition' flyer delivered to Martha and her daughter during the bedside education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached Martha's daughter 24 hours prior to discharge. Confirmed the home is prepared with groceries and meds, but the HHA intake coordinator notified us that the primary oncology nurse is out with the flu.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C Date: 11/15/2024; First Visit Date: 11/16/2024. All dates synced in Atlantis to trigger the MA workflow.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for 11/16/2024 at 09:30 AM. Physical appointment card with the MA's name and the Care Coordinator's phone number was handed to Martha.
narrative_summary: Martha was triaged in Atlantis following post-chemo rehab, where I corrected her contact information and confirmed a home-bound goal with the SW. During the maintenance phase, I provided program education and inserted my V-card, though a delay was introduced when the HHA's lead nurse fell ill. I completed the 24-hour pulse call to Martha's daughter and successfully scheduled the MA visit within the required window to ensure a safe transition despite the HHA's staffing gap.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA Oncology Specialist reports flu; intake department is scrambling for coverage.
Atlantis data sync reveals mismatch in patient primary phone number.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA intake coordinator reports the assigned nurse is out sick and cannot perform the Start of Care on the discharge day.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated the coverage gap to the Social Worker to determine if a generalist HHA nurse could bridge the gap or if the discharge date should be adjusted.

format_2_triples.situation: Martha's daughter expressed confusion about the difference between the HHA nurse and the program's Medical Assistant.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted a family education session explaining that the MA provides an extra layer of safety within 24 hours, while the HHA handles clinical wound/medication management.

format_2_triples.situation: The facility EMR shows a different discharge date than the SW's verbal update.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Checked in with the SW to confirm the finalized date before updating the Atlantis record and scheduling the MA.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Step back and allow the Social Worker to contact the HHA to resolve the staffing shortage or find a replacement nurse.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW Boundary; the PN does not manage HHA staffing or coordinate agency-level clinical coverage.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the HHA has a confirmed Start-of-Care date and schedule the MA visit for the morning after discharge to ensure the patient is not alone during the provider's illness gap.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Complies with Stage 3 Handoff rules and the Scheduling Anchor, ensuring the PN's 'First Visit' incentive is secured through the MA.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call a different HHA that you have a personal relationship with and ask them to take the case since the current HHA has a sick nurse.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Banned Action: Suggesting specific HHA agencies to the SW or interfering with the agency selection process is a direct overstep of the PN role.
